mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.02 for 60-day forward returns in backtests spanning 2010 to 2023.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production reflect contracting manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which compress corporate profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that supports equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate structural labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of observations, or the rank correlation remains below 0.01 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 2.5% threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising retail sales indicates inventory depletion and supply chain strain, which forces corporate margin compression and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines while RSAFS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.
